title: Agentic Living Doc Harness
subtitle: Run, diagnose, repair, prove, and resume living-doc work with less user intervention.
scope: Living Doc Compositor headless Codex harness, stop-negotiation layer, skill routing, proof/evidence bundles, blocker semantics, inference traces, and local dashboard.
sourceCoverage: Created from the May 7, 2026 session on agentic living-doc loops, chemistry repair skills, TrackAndBack production-hardening proof/dashboard patterns, the objective-acceptance-shaping skill output, and GitHub epic #185 with implementation tickets #186-#193.
objective: Implement a governed standalone agentic living-doc harness that can carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion with minimal user intervention. The harness must run Codex headless outside the user's active session, persist each run as a frozen contract with state and event metadata, inspect the native headless inference logs as hard evidence, treat worker stops as raw evidence rather than trusted self-report, diagnose those stops through a separate stop-negotiation inference layer, route repairable stops through the appropriate living-doc skills, patch and render the living doc between attempts, record true blockers with reason codes and required decisions, write sanitized proof/evidence bundles with inference-trace references, and expose lifecycle state, gates, blockers, skill invocations, evidence, and trace summaries through a strong local dashboard. The work is complete only when a future run can be inspected from durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries, and the dashboard makes unresolved boundaries visible enough to distinguish resumed repair, valid closure, true block, pivot, deferral, and budget exhaustion for the living doc instance's own objective.
successCondition: All acceptance criteria in this living doc are satisfied by source-system evidence: a standalone runnable harness command, isolated run process outside current user-session inference, persisted run artifacts, native inference-log inspection, stop-negotiation output, skill-routing behavior, rendered living-doc updates, sanitized evidence bundles, local dashboard views, trace references, blocker records, and contract tests that prevent closure from trusting worker self-report or wrapper summaries.

objectiveFacets.name: Living-doc lifecycle target
objectiveFacets.description: The harness carries a living doc instance from initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's own objective completion or valid terminal state.

objectiveFacets.name: Standalone headless Codex runner
objectiveFacets.description: The harness starts a bounded Codex run outside the user's active session from a living-doc JSON objective.

objectiveFacets.name: Frozen run contract
objectiveFacets.description: Each run persists contract, state, event, skill, trace, and artifact metadata outside chat history.

objectiveFacets.name: Native inference-log evidence
objectiveFacets.description: The harness inspects native headless inference logs as hard evidence instead of relying on wrapper summaries.

objectiveFacets.name: Stop negotiation
objectiveFacets.description: Worker stops are treated as raw evidence and diagnosed by a separate inference layer.

objectiveFacets.name: Skill routing
objectiveFacets.description: Repairable stops route into living-doc skills without manual selection.

objectiveFacets.name: Living-doc repair loop
objectiveFacets.description: The harness patches and renders the living doc between attempts before resuming.

objectiveFacets.name: True block mode
objectiveFacets.description: Unrepairable stops become explicit blockers with reason codes, owning layers, required decisions, and unblock criteria.

objectiveFacets.name: Proof and evidence bundles
objectiveFacets.description: Runs write sanitized bundles with proof, gate, artifact, privacy, and trace references.

objectiveFacets.name: Strong local dashboard
objectiveFacets.description: A local dashboard exposes run state, gates, skill timeline, blockers, evidence, trace summaries, and resume or exit recommendations.

objectiveFacets.name: Closure contract
objectiveFacets.description: Closure cannot rely on the worker's self-reported stop reason or completion claim.

objectiveFacets.name: Lifecycle demo
objectiveFacets.description: A sample living-doc run proves the harness can move from initial objective-bearing state to a durable completion, true block, pivot, deferral, budget exhaustion, or repair-resumed state.

invariants.name: Dashboard is part of the objective
invariants.statement: Do not reduce the dashboard to a later reporting surface; unresolved boundaries must be visible in the first implementation objective.

invariants.name: Worker stop is raw evidence
invariants.statement: Do not trust the worker's self-reported stop reason or wrapper summary as authoritative; stop-negotiation must infer the stop from native inference logs, trace, tools, edits, proof, and doc state.

invariants.name: Standalone or invalid
invariants.statement: A harness run that depends on the active user-session inference cannot prove this objective because it can interfere with the session it is supposed to observe and repair.

invariants.name: Native logs or invalid
invariants.statement: A stop diagnosis or closure verdict is invalid unless it is grounded in native headless inference logs, not only wrapper logs or summarized output.

invariants.name: True blockers stop the loop
invariants.statement: A true blocker must produce a blocker record and dashboard-visible reason before any further implementation attempt continues.

invariants.name: Proof before closure
invariants.statement: A closure claim is invalid unless objective terms, acceptance criteria, proof state, evidence bundle, and dashboard gate state agree.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Keeps the current objective state visible before implementation begins.

stats.label: Implementation
stats.value: governed harness implemented and live-run finalized

callout.title: Objective closed by live harness proof
items: The harness has a standalone runner command that can launch codex exec --json outside the active user-session inference.

The runner now records the child Codex home, discovers native JSONL traces after --execute, writes sanitized trace-discovery metadata, and attaches native trace summaries to the run contract and state.

Run artifacts persist contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, prompt.md, Codex wrapper logs, trace discovery, native trace summaries, stop verdicts, skill routing, terminal states, evidence bundles, proof validations, and dashboard output.

Stop negotiation rejects wrapper-only or worker-self-report closure and classifies missing trace evidence, fake closure, premature handoff, repairable proof gaps, true blocks, pivots, deferrals, budget exhaustion, and proven closure.

Skill routing records the living-doc repair skills and harness actions selected for each stop verdict, including mismatch metadata when wrapper or worker claims disagree with native-trace and proof-gate inference.
Repair handovers persist unresolved objective terms, unproven acceptance criteria, before/after living-doc snapshots, render results, and next-iteration instructions.

Terminal-state handling records closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, and repair-resumed states, with blocker records for true blocks and can-resume checks that reject silent continuation.

Evidence bundles and the local dashboard expose objective refs, gate state, blockers, skill timeline, rendered doc refs, evidence artifacts, native trace summaries, privacy posture, wrapper/native mismatch, and resume or exit recommendation.

The lifecycle fixture proves fake worker closure is repaired back to repair-resumed while proven closure can close, both from durable artifacts and sanitized native trace refs rather than chat memory.

Contract tests now cover the fake live-execute boundary with a separate child process that writes native Codex-style JSONL under CODEX_HOME and proves raw trace payloads do not leak into contract evidence.

The real harness run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness executed Codex headless, attached a native trace summary, scaffolded evidence, finalized iteration 1 as closed, wrote a valid proof, wrote an evidence bundle, and rendered the local dashboard.
GitHub epic #185 and child issues #186-#193 are closed against commits c9c984c and 9069b10.

sections.title: Operating Decisions
sections.rationale: Captures the choices that define the target system before tickets or implementation slices are created.

data.name: Harness must run standalone
data.decision: The harness must run Codex headless outside the user's active session.
data.rationale: If the run depends on the same interactive session that is supervising it, the harness can interfere with the inference it is meant to observe and repair.
notes: This is an objective constraint, not an implementation preference.

data.name: Native inference logs are required
data.decision: Stop diagnosis, repair routing, blocker records, and closure checks must inspect native headless inference logs.
data.rationale: Wrapper summaries can make a run look coherent while hiding the actual reasoning and tool evidence that explain why the worker stopped.
notes: Wrapper output may be useful context, but it is not proof.

data.name: Dashboard is a first-class objective surface
data.decision: The first implementation must include a strong local dashboard, not only dashboard-ready JSON.
data.rationale: The dashboard is how unresolved boundaries, blockers, traces, and proof gates become visible enough for reduced user intervention.
notes: This tightens the earlier objective that allowed a dashboard-ready data surface.

data.name: Stop diagnosis is separate from the worker
data.decision: The worker should run naturally; a separate stop-negotiation inference layer diagnoses why it stopped.
data.rationale: Prompting the worker to produce clean stop reasons may create premature stop behavior.
notes: The worker stop is evidence, not a trusted classification.

data.name: Use TrackAndBack proof harness as pattern
data.decision: Borrow the durable contract, state, event, evidence bundle, governance verdict, dashboard gate, and native trace ideas from TrackAndBack without copying its parser-specific proof domain.
data.rationale: TrackAndBack proves the value of dashboard-visible proof gates and true blocker records around headless iterative inference.
notes: /Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/specs/production_hardening_worker.md
/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/dashboard/backend/src/productionHardeningRuns.js
/Users/rene/projects/trackandback-parser-mvp/src/tb_parser_mvp/production_hardening_worker.py

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines objective-tight closure gates. Every criterion maps to a phrase in the objective and names the proof needed for acceptance.

data.name: Standalone lifecycle runner starts from a living doc
data.objectiveTerm: carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; run Codex headless outside the user's active session
data.criterion: A standalone command or script can start a bounded headless Codex run from a living-doc JSON objective without depending on the current interactive Codex session.
data.proofRequired: Runnable standalone harness command, sample input living doc, isolated process evidence, persisted run state, and captured Codex turn/log references.
data.acceptanceTest: A reviewer can run the command against a sample living doc in a separate process and see a new run directory with contract, state, events, artifacts, native trace refs, and no dependency on chat memory.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs creates standalone run directories and can launch codex exec --json outside the active session. The live run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness executed Codex headless with exitCode 0 and persisted wrapper logs, last message, and run state outside chat memory. The native trace summary traces/rollout-2026-05-07T17-33-58-019e01c9-6ef6-7171-a471-f07d59f9aba9.summary.json is attached to the run.

data.name: Each run has frozen contract and state metadata
data.objectiveTerm: persist each run as a frozen contract with state and event metadata
data.criterion: Each harness run writes durable contract, state, events, skill invocation, trace, and artifact metadata outside chat history.
data.proofRequired: .living-doc-runs/<run-id>/contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, skill-invocations.jsonl, codex-turns, and artifacts with deterministic schema or contract tests.
data.acceptanceTest: Deleting chat history does not prevent a reviewer from reconstructing run objective, stage, stop, skill actions, proof status, and next action from the run directory.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs writes contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, prompt.md, codex-turn artifact paths, and native trace refs. scripts/living-doc-harness-iteration.mjs adds iteration evidence, stop verdict, handover, terminal, proof, validation, evidence bundle, dashboard, and finalized event artifacts. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs cover the durable artifact spine.

data.name: Native inference logs are inspected
data.objectiveTerm: inspect the native headless inference logs as hard evidence; durable artifacts and native inference traces rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries
data.criterion: The harness locates and reads native headless inference logs for each run and records sanitized trace references and stop-evidence summaries.
data.proofRequired: Native trace discovery implementation, fixture or sample native log, sanitized trace summary, evidence bundle trace refs, and a contract test that fails when only wrapper output is available.
data.acceptanceTest: A sample run with a misleading wrapper summary is diagnosed from native inference-log evidence, and a run without native trace evidence cannot be accepted as closed.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-trace-reader.mjs discovers, summarizes, and attaches native Codex JSONL traces without copying private payloads. The live run has native trace hash sha256:6a88f71843c724c455ce58178a6fb5d28f996f75fc38ed4ba366049a49a121c8, 264 trace lines, source exec, cliVersion 0.128.0, and rawPayloadIncluded false.

data.name: Stop-negotiation layer diagnoses worker stops
data.objectiveTerm: treat worker stops as raw evidence, diagnose those stops through a separate stop-negotiation inference layer
data.criterion: The worker's final message is not trusted as the stop reason; a separate layer reads stop evidence and emits an inferred stop verdict.
data.proofRequired: Stop-negotiation module or skill call that consumes final message, native inference logs, trace refs, tool calls, edits, proof results, and living-doc before/after state.
data.acceptanceTest: A synthetic premature handoff is classified from trace evidence as repairable or resumable even when the worker's final message asks the user for a decision.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-stop-negotiation.mjs infers repairable, resumable, closure-candidate, closed, true-block, pivot, deferred, and budget-exhausted verdicts from proof gates, objective state, worker evidence, wrapper mismatch, next actions, and terminal signals. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-stop-negotiation.spec.mjs covers fake closure, premature handoff, and terminal true-block cases.

data.name: Stop verdicts route to living-doc skills
data.objectiveTerm: route repairable stops through the appropriate living-doc skills
data.criterion: The harness maps stop verdicts and stages to shaping, readiness, balance, repair, closure, blocker, or resume actions without manual skill selection.
data.proofRequired: Routing table or implementation plus tests for objective ambiguity, not executable, out of balance, premature handoff, closure candidate, proof failure, and true block.
data.acceptanceTest: Given representative stop verdict fixtures, the harness invokes or recommends the correct skill/action and records that routing in skill-invocations.jsonl.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-skill-router.mjs maps stop verdicts to living-doc skills and harness actions, writes skill-invocations.jsonl, and persists repair handovers with before/after doc snapshots and render results when supplied. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-skill-router.spec.mjs covers repair, resume, closure-candidate, true-block, render, and handover paths.

data.name: Living doc is patched and rendered between attempts
data.objectiveTerm: patch and render the living doc between attempts
data.criterion: When a repair skill changes the living doc, the harness persists before/after JSON, renders the updated HTML, and resumes from the repaired state.
data.proofRequired: Before/after living-doc artifacts, rendered HTML path, render command result, and resume handover linking unresolved false terms to the next run.
data.acceptanceTest: A repairable sample run changes the living doc, produces a rendered HTML update, records the patch in events, and resumes from the updated objective state.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-skill-router.mjs persists before/after living-doc snapshots, unresolved objective terms, unproven criteria, skill actions, wrapper/native mismatch metadata, and render results for repaired after-docs. The live run changed and rendered docs/living-doc-agentic-harness.json/html, then iteration finalization wrote a closed terminal state, proof artifact, evidence bundle, and dashboard.

data.name: True blockers are explicit and terminal
data.objectiveTerm: record true blockers with reason codes and required decisions
data.criterion: A true block stops the loop and writes a blocker record with stage, reason code, owning layer, required decision/source/proof, issue or follow-up reference, and acceptance criteria to unblock.
data.proofRequired: Blocker schema, blocker fixture or sample run, dashboard blocker display, and test preventing silent resume after a true block.
data.acceptanceTest: When a required source, proof authority, permission, objective decision, privacy boundary, or platform capability is missing, the harness records a true block and does not continue implementation.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-terminal-state.mjs writes true-blocked terminal records, blocker JSON/JSONL artifacts, and can-resume checks that reject silent continuation. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-terminal-state.spec.mjs covers missing-source, missing-permission, missing-proof-authority, objective-undecidable, privacy-boundary, platform-capability-gap, budget-exhausted, pivot, deferred, closed, and repair-resumed states.

data.name: Sanitized proof and evidence bundles are written
data.objectiveTerm: write sanitized proof/evidence bundles with inference-trace references
data.criterion: Every terminal or dashboard-visible harness run writes a sanitized evidence bundle and summary.
data.proofRequired: evidence/living-doc-harness/<run-id>/bundle.json and summary.md containing run hashes, objective hash, gates, proof commands, skill verdicts, stop verdict, rendered doc refs, privacy flags, and trace refs.
data.acceptanceTest: The evidence bundle omits raw private payloads and raw logs while preserving enough hashes and summaries to verify what happened.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.mjs writes living-doc-harness-evidence-bundle/v1 bundle.json plus summary.md with run hashes, objective/source hashes, proof gates, stop verdict, terminal state, blockers, skill timeline, trace refs, rendered doc refs, artifacts, recommendation, and privacy flags. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.spec.mjs proves private trace payloads are omitted.

data.name: Strong local dashboard exposes unresolved boundaries
data.objectiveTerm: expose the run state, gates, blockers, skill invocations, evidence, and trace summaries through a strong local dashboard
data.criterion: A local dashboard page lists harness runs and shows objective, stage, gates, stop-negotiation verdict, skill timeline, blockers, evidence bundle, trace summary, privacy status, and resume or exit recommendation for each run.
data.proofRequired: Dashboard route/page, local data/API reader for run directories and evidence bundles, and a populated sample run visible in the UI.
data.acceptanceTest: A reviewer can inspect a run from the dashboard and identify whether it should resume, repair, close, block, pivot, defer, or stop from budget exhaustion.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.mjs renders a standalone local dashboard over run directories and evidence bundles. The live run wrote evidence/living-doc-harness/ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness/bundle.json and docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard.html. The bundle recommendation is close, lifecycleStage is closed, stopVerdict is objective-proven, and privacy flags omit raw prompt, wrapper log, native trace, and message content.

data.name: Closure cannot trust worker self-report or wrapper summary
data.objectiveTerm: rather than chat memory or wrapper summaries; without relying on the worker's self-reported stop reason
data.criterion: Closure requires objective-term conservation, acceptance criteria proof, evidence bundle, dashboard gate agreement, native trace evidence, and stop-negotiation verdict rather than the worker's final message or wrapper summary.
data.proofRequired: Contract tests or fixtures that fail when the worker or wrapper claims done but acceptance, native trace evidence, proof, evidence, or objective terms remain unresolved.
data.acceptanceTest: A fake worker completion claim with missing acceptance proof is rejected as closure and shown as closure-candidate, repair-first, blocked, or unsatisfied in the dashboard.
data.currentProof: tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.spec.mjs runs a fake worker completion claim with unresolved objective terms and unproven acceptance criteria. The harness classifies it as closure-candidate, writes a repair-resumed terminal state, and validates that a fabricated closed proof is rejected.

data.name: Lifecycle demo is inspectable from durable artifacts and native traces
data.objectiveTerm: carry a living doc instance from its initial objective-bearing state toward that instance's objective completion; distinguish resumed repair, valid closure, true block, pivot, deferral, and budget exhaustion for the living doc instance's own objective
data.criterion: At least one sample living-doc harness run starts from an initial objective-bearing doc and reaches a valid closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, or repair-resumed state inspectable from artifacts, native traces, and dashboard without reading chat history.
data.proofRequired: Sample living doc, run directory, native trace refs, evidence bundle, rendered output, dashboard view, and verification command showing the sample lifecycle state is reproducible.
data.acceptanceTest: A reviewer can start from the dashboard or evidence bundle and reconstruct the instance objective, lifecycle stage, stop, native-trace evidence, skill routing, proof state, blocker or closure verdict, and next action.
data.currentProof: scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-fixture.mjs creates two reproducible sample runs from tests/fixtures/minimal-doc.json: one fake closure repaired back to repair-resumed and one proven closure that reaches closed. The fixture writes run directories, sanitized trace summaries, skill routing handovers, terminal states, iteration proof artifacts, evidence bundles, and a dashboard.

sections.title: Implementation Surfaces
sections.rationale: Names the bounded implementation lanes that must exist for the objective to become real.

data.name: Standalone headless runner command
data.summary: Command surface creates a durable run directory, state, event log, prompt, prepared codex exec command, and live --execute child process boundary outside the active user-session inference. Post-execute trace discovery attaches sanitized native trace summaries back to contract/state artifacts.

codePaths.range: new harness command
standalone run directory and codex exec command boundary
runner contract test without live Codex dependency

notes: Does not depend on chat memory for objective state.
Does not use the current interactive Codex session as the worker process.
The contract test exercises a separate fake Codex child process that writes native Codex-style JSONL under CODEX_HOME.

data.name: Run contract, state, and events
data.summary: The persisted run and iteration proof surfaces now have schema, validator, runner artifacts, state, events, skill invocations, trace refs, handovers, terminal states, evidence bundles, dashboard refs, and finalizer events.

codePaths.range: target artifact shape
iteration proof handover schema
runtime validator and CLI
contract fixtures and closure rejection tests

notes: Modeled after TrackAndBack production-hardening run directories, adapted for living-doc objectives.
This completes the contract-proven artifact spine; live-run evidence remains a separate proof boundary.

data.name: Native inference-log reader
data.summary: Reads native Codex session JSONL files, emits sanitized trace summaries, attaches native trace refs back to a harness run contract/state, feeds evidence bundles and the iteration finalizer, and is now invoked automatically by the runner after --execute.
notes: Wrapper logs and final messages are context, not proof.
Privacy boundaries require trace references, hashes, and summaries rather than raw private logs in shared bundles.
Runner trace-discovery.json hashes Codex home and candidate paths instead of exposing raw trace locations.

codePaths.range: discover, summarize, and attach native Codex traces
privacy-safe summary and run-attachment tests

data.name: Stop-negotiation inference layer
data.summary: Post-run diagnostic layer now infers typed stop verdicts from native trace refs, worker evidence, wrapper claims, proof gates, objective state, available next actions, and terminal signals.
notes: Worker self-report is not authoritative.
Wrapper summaries are not authoritative.
Do not over-prompt the worker to produce clean stop reasons.

codePaths.range: verdict inference and CLI
fake closure, premature handoff, true block, and CLI fixtures

data.name: Skill router
data.summary: Maps stop verdicts to living-doc skills and harness actions, writes skill-invocations.jsonl, persists repair handovers with unresolved objective terms and unproven acceptance criteria, snapshots before/after docs, and renders after-docs when supplied.
notes: Should cover objective-acceptance-shaping, objective-execution-readiness, balance scan, equilibrium rebalance, catalytic repair, conservation audit, activation review, and reaction path validation.

codePaths.range: verdict-to-skill routing and handover persistence
repair, resume, closure-candidate, true-block, render, and handover tests

data.name: Evidence bundle writer
data.summary: Writes sanitized bundle JSON and summary markdown for harness runs, including objective hash, source hash, lifecycle stage, proof gates, stop verdict, terminal state, blockers, skill timeline, trace refs, rendered doc refs, artifacts, recommendation, and privacy flags.

codePaths.range: target artifact shape

data.summary: Local static HTML dashboard renders sanitized bundle data for one or more run directories and exposes lifecycle state, proof gates, blockers, skill timeline, native trace summaries, privacy status, objective refs, rendered doc refs, evidence artifact refs, wrapper/native mismatch, and resume or exit recommendation.
notes: This is in scope for the first implementation, not deferred reporting.
The dashboard now makes the mismatch between wrapper claims and native/proof inference visible.

data.name: True-block and terminal-state contracts
data.summary: Defines and writes durable terminal states that stop the loop without pretending the objective is complete: closed, true-blocked, pivoted, deferred, budget-exhausted, and repair-resumed. True blockers produce dashboard-visible blocker records and can-resume checks reject silent continuation.
notes: Terminal state handling is part of the lifecycle proof, not a follow-up reporting layer.

codePaths.range: terminal-state writer, blocker records, and can-resume gate

data.name: Lifecycle fixture and closure proof
data.summary: Runs the harness pieces together against a sample living doc. The fixture creates a fake-closure run and a proven-closure run, attaches sanitized native trace summaries, infers stop verdicts, routes skills, writes terminal states, emits iteration proof artifacts, writes evidence bundles, renders the dashboard, and proves that worker self-report alone cannot close the objective. The runner contract now separately proves post-execute trace attachment from a child process.

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Defines the proof escalation path from shaped objective to runnable harness and dashboard-visible closure.

data.name: Rung 1 - objective shaped
data.evidence: This living doc carries the tightened objective and objective-bound acceptance criteria.
data.nextStep: No objective-shaping repair is currently needed.

data.name: Rung 2 - contracts and schemas
data.evidence: Iteration proof handover schema and validator exist at scripts/living-doc-harness-contract-schema.json and scripts/validate-living-doc-harness-contract.mjs, with tests in tests/contract/living-doc-harness-contract.spec.mjs. The iteration finalizer writes proof and validation artifacts.
data.nextStep: Closed for this harness objective; use the same contract for future living-doc instances.

data.name: Rung 3 - standalone headless runner
data.evidence: scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs can create a standalone run directory with contract.json, state.json, events.jsonl, prompt.md, and a prepared codex exec --json command. The live run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness executed a separate Codex child process with exitCode 0, captured wrapper logs and last message, and attached native trace summary sha256:6a88f71843c724c455ce58178a6fb5d28f996f75fc38ed4ba366049a49a121c8.
data.nextStep: Use the operator command against future living docs as runs accumulate; closure remains governed by each instance's proof gates.

data.name: Rung 4 - stop diagnosis and repair loop
data.evidence: Native trace discovery and sanitized summary attachment exist in scripts/living-doc-harness-trace-reader.mjs and are called by scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs after --execute. Stop-negotiation verdict inference exists in scripts/living-doc-harness-stop-negotiation.mjs. Skill routing and handover persistence exist in scripts/living-doc-harness-skill-router.mjs. Terminal records exist in scripts/living-doc-harness-terminal-state.mjs.
data.nextStep: Closed for this harness objective; future instances still need their own proof-gated stop verdict.

data.name: Rung 5 - dashboard and evidence
data.evidence: scripts/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.mjs writes sanitized evidence bundles and renders a local static dashboard over run directories. The live run wrote evidence/living-doc-harness/ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness/bundle.json with lifecycleStage closed and recommendation close, and rendered docs/living-doc-harness-dashboard.html.
data.nextStep: Use dashboard artifacts as the inspection surface for future live runs.

data.name: Rung 6 - lifecycle proof
data.evidence: scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle-fixture.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.spec.mjs run the sample doc through fake-closure repair and proven closure. scripts/living-doc-harness-iteration.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs prove the evidence-template command and finalizer. tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs additionally proves a separate execute child process produces native trace summaries for durable inspection.
data.nextStep: Run future objectives through the same governed command surfaces and inspect them from artifacts, not chat memory.

sections.title: Dashboard Proof Surface
sections.rationale: Keeps the dashboard-specific proof needs visible because the dashboard is part of the objective.

data.name: Run list
data.currentCoverage: Static dashboard renders run id, objective ref, lifecycle stage, status, stop verdict, recommendation, rendered doc refs, evidence artifacts, and blockers from sanitized evidence bundles. The lifecycle fixture renders both a repair-resumed fake-closure run and a closed proven-closure run.
data.nextStep: Use the dashboard as the local inspection surface for future harness runs.

data.name: Gate strip
data.currentCoverage: Dashboard renders proof gates from the evidence bundle, including standalone run, native trace, rendering, skill routing, terminal state, blockers, evidence bundle, and closure posture. The lifecycle fixture proves the gate strip distinguishes repair-resumed from closed.
data.nextStep: Keep gate names stable as more terminal states accumulate.

data.name: Skill timeline
data.currentCoverage: Evidence bundle reads skill-invocations.jsonl and dashboard lists skill name, status, stop classification, and associated handover artifact refs.
data.nextStep: Add richer per-skill patch visualization only if future repair runs need it.

data.name: Native trace summary
data.currentCoverage: Evidence bundle and dashboard list sanitized native trace summary refs, trace hashes, line counts, and privacy flags without raw native trace payloads.
data.nextStep: Keep validating raw prompt/message/tool payloads are omitted.

data.name: Wrapper versus native evidence
data.currentCoverage: Dashboard surfaces wrapper/native mismatch metadata from stop verdicts and repair handovers, making fake closure and premature handoff boundaries visible.
data.nextStep: Use mismatch display when auditing future stopped runs.

sections.title: Blockers And Boundaries
sections.rationale: Keeps possible true-block cases visible so they are not hidden as vague future work or premature user handoff.

data.name: Source unavailable
data.riskSummary: A run may require a repo, file, issue, MCP tool, or external source that is unavailable to the harness.
data.nextCheck: Verify missing-source blocker behavior in a live headless run when a required source is unavailable.
evidence: Derived from TrackAndBack source material and proof authority gates.

data.name: Objective undecidable
data.riskSummary: Some living docs may not have enough context to shape an accountable objective without user decision.
data.nextCheck: Exercise objective-undecidable terminal state in a live headless run where the objective cannot be decided from available context.
evidence: Derived from the new objective-acceptance-shaping skill.

data.name: Premature handoff
data.riskSummary: The worker may ask for user input even when a skill, source check, or next action is available.
data.nextCheck: Verify premature-handoff classification against a live headless run that asks for user input while a harness action remains available.
evidence: Derived from the session decision not to over-prompt the worker for stop reasons.

data.name: Privacy boundary
data.riskSummary: Native inference traces can include private context that should not be shared in evidence bundles.
data.nextCheck: Keep validating that live native traces never leak raw prompt, message, reasoning, or tool payloads into bundles or dashboards.
evidence: Borrowed from TrackAndBack sanitized evidence bundle practice.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-evidence-dashboard.spec.mjs and tests/contract/living-doc-harness-iteration.spec.mjs assert private trace payloads are omitted.

data.name: Live headless proof boundary
data.riskSummary: The live proof boundary for this harness objective is closed: a real --execute run produced native trace evidence, finalized iteration 1 as closed, wrote a valid proof, emitted an evidence bundle, and rendered the dashboard. Future living-doc instances still need instance-specific proof before their own closure claims.
data.nextCheck: For future bounded living docs, run --execute, scaffold/finalize iteration evidence, and inspect the local dashboard before any closure claim.
evidence: tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs exercises a separate fake Codex child process writing native Codex-style JSONL under CODEX_HOME.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.spec.mjs verifies repair-resumed and closed dashboard states from durable artifacts.

objective-conservation-audit on 2026-05-07 identified live proof as remaining mass; the real run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness closed that mass with native trace sha256:6a88f71843c724c455ce58178a6fb5d28f996f75fc38ed4ba366049a49a121c8 and valid iteration proof.
evidence/living-doc-harness/ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness/bundle.json reports lifecycleStage closed, recommendation close, and stopVerdict objective-proven.

sections.title: Source Anchors
sections.rationale: Records the concrete source surfaces and prior pages that should guide implementation.

data.name: Agentic harness loop presentation
data.summary: Static presentation of the run, stop, skill repair, patch, proof, resume, and exit loop.

data.name: Proof dashboard layer presentation
data.summary: Static presentation of frozen contracts, state events, evidence bundles, true blocks, traceability, dashboard gates, and stop-negotiation.

data.name: TrackAndBack production hardening worker
data.summary: Reference implementation for blocker records, governance verdicts, sanitized evidence bundles, and proof gates.

data.name: TrackAndBack dashboard run gates
data.summary: Reference dashboard data layer that derives gates, blockers, layers, and evidence posture from run artifacts.

data.name: TrackAndBack native inference logs
data.summary: Reference for separating native headless inference logs from wrapper output so proof can inspect the hard run evidence.

sections.title: Explainability
sections.rationale: Keeps a short plain-language statement of what the living doc is trying to make true.

data.name: Plain state
data.objectiveExplanation: This doc is about building a standalone governed loop that can take one living doc from its starting objective state toward that doc's real completion while recording every stop, repair, proof claim, and blocker outside chat memory.
data.currentStateExplanation: This living doc objective is closed by a live headless run. The run ldh-20260507T113500Z-doc-living-doc-agentic-harness executed Codex outside the active session, attached a sanitized native trace summary, finalized iteration 1 as closed, wrote a valid proof and evidence bundle, and rendered the dashboard. Future living docs still need their own instance-specific proof before closure.
